svg.title: AI pipeline diagram for the economic dashboard project
svg.desc: Two pipelines: fred.py processes FRED economic indicators through a filter agent and a deep agent, while articles.py runs three parallel article agents, each with its own Firecrawl tool. Outputs are saved to the frontend's indicators and articles folders.
svg.text: Two backends (fred.py + articles.py) generate HTML/PNG reports that sync to the frontend
save filtered set back to Mongo
2. articles.py — News Article Pipeline (3 parallel agents, each with its own tool)

g.text: FRED API pulls CPI, PPI, GDP, unemployment, interest rate → raw rows land in MongoDB.
Filter agent calls @tool query, keeps only the important indicators (data was too large), and writes the slim set back to Mongo.
The filtered dataset is uploaded into a Daytona sandbox; the deep agent runs analysis there and emits an HTML report plus PNG charts.
Backend downloads sandbox files into indicators/{name}/{*.html, *.png} and they auto-sync to the frontend.
In parallel, articles.py runs three topic agents — each with its own Firecrawl @tool (search + scraping) — and each writes its own HTML report.
